name: 20-enhanced-bash
description: Phase 6.9 backfill #164: exercises the Enhanced Bash tool (Phase 3.9). The shared bilateral flow is intentionally minimal (1-turn no-tool, no harness tools registered) because the load-bearing surface here is the OR-side production `run_command` tool's spawn-and-supervise machinery (timeout → SIGTERM → 250ms grace → SIGKILL; clamp at MAX_TIMEOUT_MS=600_000 firing a `warn` notify). The Claude Agent SDK's built-in `Bash` tool has its own equivalent (timeout knob + SIGTERM-on-timeout), but its child-process is spawned inside the subprocess CLI and is structurally inaccessible from this harness — there is no way to observe its exit code / stderr suffix / kill-grace timing from the agent SDK's user-facing event stream (only the model-visible string output). Bilateral parity for the Phase 3.9 spawn machinery is therefore structurally impossible with the current harness shape (see PR body ambiguity call #1). The load-bearing assertions live in the companion OR-only supplemental driver in `scenarios.comparative.test.ts`, which constructs an inline OR run with the production `runCommandTool()` registered, scripts two `run_command` tool-use turns plus a closing text turn, and asserts: (a) `run_command({command:'sleep 5', timeout_ms:100, description:'list build artifacts'})` returns within a generous timing envelope (100ms timeout + 250ms SIGKILL grace + slow-CI jitter) with `exitCode === 1` and `stderr` containing the canonical `terminated by SIGTERM` or `terminated by SIGKILL` suffix from `run-command.ts:139-145`; (b) `run_command({command:':', timeout_ms:700000})` runs to completion (the clamp shortens the budget to MAX_TIMEOUT_MS, but `:` exits immediately so no SIGTERM fires) and the `Notification` hook receives the canonical `'run_command timeout_ms exceeds MAX_TIMEOUT_MS, clamping'` warn payload from `run-command.ts:54-57`. The `description` advisory is observed via the captured `tool_call.input.description` event projection on the OR side (the schema's free-text field round-trips from the model's scripted args through the canonical event stream); deeper propagation into a recorded per-request log artifact is `persistSession: false` here so out-of-scope, documented as ambiguity call #3 in the PR body. The issue body's claim that `RunCommandResult` carries `truncated: true` + `exitCode: null` on the kill path is INCORRECT against the actual production interface at `src/tools/run-command.ts:11-15` (`{exitCode: number, stdout: string, stderr: string}` — no truncated flag, never-null exitCode); the supplemental asserts the actually-observable shape and the PR body documents the spec mismatch as ambiguity call #2 with a TODO for a structural follow-up.
prompt: Confirm the enhanced bash tool is registered for this session.
systemPrompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer concisely.

content.text: Enhanced bash tool is registered for this session.
Enhanced bash tool is registered for this session.
Listing build artifacts via run_command with a tight 100ms timeout.
Now invoking with an over-MAX_TIMEOUT_MS budget to exercise the clamp.
Done — the first call hit the timeout and was killed; the second clamped to MAX_TIMEOUT_MS and completed.
